description: TelemetryDeck TQL queries for the "hapticCapabilities" signal of Basic Root Checker. The signal fires once per cold start (RootHaptics.init) and carries five string parameters: hasVibrator, amplitudeControl, primitiveClick, envelopeEffectsSupported (all "true"/"false") and sdkInt (the Android API level, e.g. "33"). The signal name lives in the "type" dimension; unique devices are counted with a thetaSketch over clientUser. Because the parameter values are strings, selectors match "true"/"false", not booleans. Omit "intervals" to let the dashboard date picker drive the range, or add e.g. "intervals": ["P90D/PT0S"] to hardcode a window. Swap the thetaSketch over clientUser for {"type":"count","name":"count"} to count signals (~cold starts) instead of unique devices.

parameters.hasVibrator: "true"/"false" — device has an actuator at all
parameters.primitiveClick: "true"/"false" — API 30+ crisp PRIMITIVE_CLICK for UI taps (else a raw amplitude one-shot)
parameters.sdkInt: Android API level as a string, e.g. "33"

queries.name: Capability support overview
queries.description: One panel, all four flags. totalUsers is the denominator (everyone who emitted the signal); each filtered aggregation is the count of unique devices where that capability is on.

queries.name: Single-flag breakdown (template)
queries.description: True vs false split for one boolean param. Swap "hasVibrator" for amplitudeControl, primitiveClick, or envelopeEffectsSupported.

queries.name: Android version (SDK level) distribution
queries.description: Unique devices per Android API level. Note sdkInt is a string, so ordering is lexical.

queries.name: Envelope (PWLE ramp) adoption over time
queries.description: The rich "checking" sweep is the rarest capability — this tracks the supported share month over month against the total.

queries.name: Tap-quality matrix
queries.description: Cross-tabs primitiveClick x amplitudeControl to bucket devices into crisp-primitive vs raw-amplitude vs no-tap — the fallback ladder in RootHaptics.playTap.
